title: teucrium heterophyllum subsp. brevipilosum
intro: Teucrium heterophyllum subsp. brevipilosum, also known as the Himalayan germander, is a plant species belonging to the genus Teucrium, which is a group of herbs and shrubs commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Teucrium heterophyllum subsp. brevipilosum include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
